sections.conclusion: Donation of surplus funds to charitable organizations is permitted under section 85807(c) so long as no substantial part of the proceeds will have a material financial effect on the former candidate, any member of his or her immediate family, or his or her campaign treasurer. A fund established to aid families of deceased law enforcement officers would appear to be the type of charitable organization described in Section 85807(c).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a candidate can donate surplus campaign funds to a charitable fund that compensates families of police officers killed in the line of duty under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC confirms that donating surplus campaign funds to a charitable fund for families of deceased law enforcement officers is permitted under Section 85807(c) of the Political Reform Act, provided the donation has no material financial effect on the candidate, their family, or campaign treasurer.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is written confirmation of prior telephone advice. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers present, but content clearly delineates the legal question and FPPC's response. Document is letter format confirming previous oral advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a candidate can donate surplus campaign funds to a charitable fund that compensates families of police officers killed in the line of duty under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Donation of surplus funds to charitable organizations is permitted under section 85807(c) so long as no substantial part of the proceeds will have a material financial effect on the former candidate, any member of his or her immediate family, or his or her campaign treasurer. A fund established to aid families of deceased law enforcement officers would appear to be the type of charitable organization described in Section 85807(c).

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephonic advice that a candidate may donate surplus campaign funds to a charitable organization that compensates families of police officers killed in the line of duty, consistent with Section 85807(c) of the Political Reform Act.
